1DFFT.NumGridK.description: The energy region from 0 to 1DFFT.EnergyCutoff is discretized using the number of grid points specified by the keyword 1DFFT.NumGridK .

1DFFT.NumGridR.description: Specify the number of radial grid points in real space by the keyword 1DFFT.NumGridR .

Atoms.Cont.Orbitals.description: Using the serial numbers of atoms defined in the first column of the keyword Atoms.SpeciesAndCoordinates , specify the atoms for which the optimized basis is to be output by the keyword Atoms.Cont.Orbitals as follows.

Atoms.SpeciesAndCoordinates.description: Specify the atomic coordinates and the number of electrons for each spin by the keyword Atoms.SpeciesAndCoordinates as in the following example.

Atoms.SpeciesAndCoordinates.Unit.description: Specify the unit of atomic coordinates with the keyword Atoms.SpeciesAndCoordinates.Unit .

Atoms.UnitVectors.description: Specify the unit-cell vectors 𝐚 , 𝐛 , and 𝐜 by the keyword Atoms.UnitVectors as follows.

Atoms.UnitVectors.Unit.description: Specify the unit of the vectors in the unit cell by the keyword Atoms.UnitVectors.Unit .

Band.KPath.UnitCell.description: Specify the unit-cell vectors used in band-dispersion calculations with the keyword Band.KPath.UnitCell as in the following example.

Band.Nkpath.description: Specify the number of paths in the band-dispersion calculation by Band.Nkpath .

Band.kpath.description: Specify the band-dispersion paths with the keyword Band.kpath as follows.

CntOrb.fileout.description: If you want to output the optimized radial functions to files, the keyword CntOrb.fileout must be set to ON .

DATA.PATH.description: Specify the path to the VPS and PAO directories as follows.

Definition.of.Atomic.Species.description: Please specify each atomic species by explicitly giving the file names of both the pseudo-atomic basis functions and the pseudopotential.

Dos.Erange.description: Specify the energy range in the DOS calculation with the keyword Dos.Erange as follows.

Dos.Kgrid.description: To perform the DOS calculation, specify the grid points (n1, n2, n3) for discretizing the first Brillouin zone with the keyword Dos.Kgrid .

Hubbard.U.values.description: Define the effective U value for the orbitals of each atomic species by the following keyword.

MD.Fixed.XYZ.description: By specifying the following keyword, arbitrary atomic coordinates can be fixed to their initial values during structural optimization and molecular-dynamics simulations.

MD.Init.Velocity.description: In molecular-dynamics simulations, initial velocities can be assigned to each atom by the following description.

MD.Opt.DIIS.History.description: When structural optimization is performed using DIIS , EF , or RF , the keyword MD.Opt.DIIS.History specifies the number of past steps referred to for structural optimization.

MD.Opt.StartDIIS.description: Specify the step at which structural optimization by DIIS , EF , or RF starts with the keyword MD.Opt.StartDIIS .

MD.Opt.criterion.description: If a structural-optimization method is selected by the keyword MD.Type , set its convergence criterion (Hartree/Bohr) with the keyword MD.Opt.criterion .

MD.TempControl.description: The keyword MD.TempControl specifies the temperature of atomic motion in molecular-dynamics calculations for the NVT ensemble.

MD.TimeStep.description: Specify the time step (fs) with the keyword MD.TimeStep .

MD.maxIter.description: Specify the maximum number of iterations in MD calculations or structural optimization calculations with the keyword MD.maxIter .

MO.Nkpoint.description: If MO.fileout=ON and scf.EigenvalueSolver=Band are set, specify the number of

NH.Mass.HeatBath.description: If NVT_NH is selected by the keyword MD.Type , this keyword sets the mass of the heat bath.

Num.CntOrb.Atoms.description: Specify the number of atoms for which the optimized radial functions are to be output by the keyword Num.CntOrb.Atoms .

System.Name.description: This keyword specifies the file name of the output files.

k.description: points at which MOs are output with the keyword MO.kpoint as follows.

level.of.fileout.description: This keyword controls the level of information written to output files.

level.of.stdout.description: This keyword controls the level of information printed to standard output.

num.HOMOs.description: Specify the number of highest occupied molecular orbitals (HOMOs) to be output with the keyword num.HOMOs .

num.LUMOs.description: Specify the number of lowest unoccupied molecular orbitals (LUMOs) to be output with the keyword num.LUMOs .

orbitalOpt.HistoryPulay.description: In the quasi-Newton EF and DIIS methods, the number of past steps referred to in order to estimate the contraction coefficients at the next step is specified by the keyword orbitalOpt.HistoryPulay .

orbitalOpt.Opt.Method.description: Two methods are supported as convergence methods for orbital optimization.

orbitalOpt.Opt.maxIter.description: Specify the maximum number of orbital-optimization iterations with the keyword orbitalOpt.Opt.maxIter .

orbitalOpt.SD.step.description: The steepest-descent method is applied in the optimization steps before the quasi-Newton EF and DIIS methods start.

orbitalOpt.StartPulay.description: The quasi-Newton EF or DIIS method starts from the optimization step specified by the keyword orbitalOpt.StartPulay .

orbitalOpt.criterion.description: Specify the convergence criterion for orbital optimization ((Hartree/bohr) 2 ) with the keyword orbitalOpt.criterion .

orbitalOpt.scf.maxIter.description: Specify the maximum number of SCF iterations in orbital optimization with the keyword orbitalOpt.scf.maxIter .

orderN.Exact.Inverse.S.description: If the keyword orderN.Exact.Inverse.S is set to on , the inverse of the overlap matrix of each truncated cluster is evaluated exactly.

orderN.Expand.Core.description: If the keyword orderN.Expand.Core is set to on , the core region consists of atoms within a sphere of radius 1.2 × r min .

orderN.KrylovH.order.description: Specify the dimension of the Krylov subspace for the Hamiltonian of each truncated cluster by the keyword orderN.KrylovH.order .

orderN.KrylovS.order.description: If orderN.Exact.Inverse.S=off is set by the keyword described later, the inverse of the overlap matrix is approximated by the Krylov subspace method.

orderN.Recalc.Buffer.description: If the keyword orderN.Recalc.Buffer is set to on , the buffer matrix is recalculated at every SCF iteration.

scf.Constraint.NC.Spin.description: If you perform a constrained DFT calculation to control the noncollinear orientation of the spin magnetic moment, set the keyword scf.Constraint.NC.Spin to ON .

scf.Constraint.NC.Spin.v.description: When performing a constrained DFT calculation to control the noncollinear orientation of the spin magnetic moment, specify the prefactor (eV) of the penalty function by the keyword scf.Constraint.NC.Spin.v .

scf.EigenvalueSolver.description: Specify the method used to solve the eigenvalue problem with scf.EigenvalueSolver .

scf.Electric.Field.description: The keyword scf.Electric.Field makes it possible to introduce an external uniform electric field represented by a sawtooth waveform.

scf.ElectronicTemperature.description: Set the electronic temperature (K) by the keyword scf.ElectronicTemperature .

scf.Hubbard.Occupation.description: In the LDA+ U method, three occupation operators, onsite , full , and dual , can be selected by specifying the keyword scf.Hubbard.Occupation .

scf.Hubbard.U.description: For LDA+ U and GGA+ U calculations, set the keyword scf.Hubbard.U to ON (ON | OFF).

scf.Init.Mixing.Weight.description: Specify the initial mixing weight used in the simple mixing, GR-Pulay, RMM-DIIS, Kerker, RMM-DIISK, RMM-DIISV, and RMM-DIISH methods with the keyword scf.Init.Mixing.Weight .

scf.Kerker.factor.description: Specify the Kerker factor used in the Kerker and RMM-DIISK mixing methods.

scf.Kgrid.description: In OpenMX, the first Brillouin zone in k space is discretized by a uniform mesh.

scf.Max.Mixing.Weight.description: Specify the upper bound of the mixing weight in the simple mixing and Kerker mixing methods with scf.Max.Mixing.Weight .

scf.Min.Mixing.Weight.description: Specify the lower bound of the mixing weight in the simple mixing and Kerker mixing methods with scf.Min.Mixing.Weight .

scf.Mixing.EveryPulay.description: In Pulay-type mixing methods, the residual vectors tend to become linearly dependent on one another as the SCF steps proceed, making convergence difficult.

scf.Mixing.History.description: In GR-Pulay [ 60 ] , RMM-DIIS [ 61 ] , RMM-DIISK [ 61 ] , RMM-DIISV [ 61 ] , and RMM-DIISH [ 61 ] , the input electronic density (Hamiltonian) for the next SCF iteration step is estimated on the basis of the electronic density (Hamiltonian) from previous SCF iterations.

scf.Mixing.StartPulay.description: Specify the SCF step at which GR-Pulay, RMM-DIIS, Kerker, RMM-DIISK, RMM-DIISV, and RMM-DIISH are started with scf.Mixing.StartPulay .

scf.Mixing.Type.description: The keyword scf.Mixing.Type specifies the electronic-density mixing scheme used to generate the electronic density (or density matrix) to be input to the next iteration step of the SCF calculation.

scf.Ngrid.description: The keyword scf.Ngrid specifies the number of grid points used to discretize the

scf.ProExpn.VNA.description: To expand the neutral-atom potential VNA by projection operators [ 45 ] , set the keyword scf.ProExpn.VNA to ON ; otherwise, set it to OFF .

scf.SpinOrbit.Coupling.description: Set this keyword to ON to enable spin-orbit interaction; otherwise set it to OFF .

scf.SpinPolarization.description: Specify whether the electronic system is non-spin-polarized or spin-polarized with the keyword scf.SpinPolarization .

scf.criterion.description: Specify the convergence criterion of the SCF calculation with the keyword scf.criterion (in Hartree units).

scf.energycutoff.description: Specify the cutoff energy that determines the integration-grid spacing with the keyword scf.energycutoff .

scf.maxIter.description: Set the maximum number of SCF iterations with the keyword scf.maxIter .

scf.partialCoreCorrection.description: To perform Partial Core Correction (PCC) in the calculation of the exchange-correlation energy and its potential, specify the keyword scf.partialCoreCorrection .

scf.system.charge.description: Specify the amount of electron and hole doping with the keyword scf.system.charge .
